events.label: reasoning
events.text: The user wants me to return a single valid JSON object. The system has already instructed me in the system prompt. I need to analyze the scenario: Flashing & weeps on a commercial project, condition is flashing not lapped/sealed, rough-complete stage. Based on TMS 402 and BIA tech notes, flashing must be properly lapped and sealed to prevent water intrusion. This is a non-compliant condition. Decision must be fail because it is dangerous or non-compliant (water intrusion risk, potential structural damage, mold, etc.). Risk likely high because water intrusion in masonry veneer can cause significant damage. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete roughly 80-90% since roughed in but not closed out. Findings: flashing not lapped/sealed at joints/terminations, water infiltration path. Actions: remove/rework, clean surfaces, install/apply compatible sealant, ensure laps per manufacturer/code, inspect. Rationale: TMS 402 requires flashing to be installed with laps and sealed to drain water to exterior; observed condition violates requirements and creates water intrusion risk. References: TMS 402, BIA tech notes. No sound source, event_time, rate, order, workable. So set those to defaults. Ensure JSON valid.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision": "fail", "risk": "high", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 85, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0, "rate": 0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Flashing joints and/or terminations are not lapped and sealed as required", "Continuity of flashing system is interrupted, creating a path for water infiltration behind the masonry veneer"], "actions": ["Remove or rework affected flashing as needed", "Clean and prepare flashing surfaces for proper adhesion", "Install lapped flashing sections with minimum required overlap and seal seams/ends with compatible sealant per manufacturer and code", "Verify all end dams, terminations, and weep openings are correctly placed before closing out"], "rationale": "TMS 402 and BIA guidance require masonry flashing to be continuous, lapped, and sealed at joints and terminations to direct water to the exterior through weeps. Flashing that is not lapped/sealed creates a breach in the drainage plane, allowing water intrusion that can damage substrates, insulation, and interior finishes and may lead to mold or structural deterioration.", "references": ["TMS 402", "BIA tech notes"]}
